title: Windows Registry

headings.text: 4- Acquisition Methods with KAPE and Traditional Approaches
4-3 Live Acquisition via reg save / FTK Imager

overview: The Windows Registry is a hierarchical database critical to Windows operation, storing OS, hardware, and application configurations in a tree of keys and values. The registry functions similarly to a file system:

study.overview: The Windows Registry is a hierarchical database critical to Windows operation, storing OS, hardware, and application configurations in a tree of keys and values. The registry functions similarly to a file system:
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
Pivot through related notes, telemetry sources, and MITRE references.
Document findings, gaps, containment options, and detection improvements.

importantCommands.code:
C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\EZTools\RECmd>RECmd.exe -f c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\collection\c\windows\System32\config\SAM --sk administrator 
RECmd version 2.0.0.0

Author: Eric Zimmerman (saericzimmerman@gmail.com)
https://github.com/EricZimmerman/RECmd

Note: Enclose all strings containing spaces (and all RegEx) with double quotes

Command line: -f c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\collection\c\windows\System32\config\SAM --sk administrator 
Processing hive c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\collection\c\windows\System32\config\SAM

        Found 4 search hits in c:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\collection\c\windows\System32\config\SAM
        Key: SAM\Domains\Account\Users\Names\Administrator
        Key: SAM\Domains\Builtin\Aliases\Names\Administrators
        Key: SAM\Domains\Builtin\Aliases\Names\Hyper-V Administrators
        Key: SAM\Domains\Builtin\Aliases\Names\Storage Replica Administrators

C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\EZTools\RECmd>

rr.exe -H C:\hives\NTUSER.DAT -o ntuser_report.txt
rip.exe -r C:\hives\NTUSER.DAT -p userassist > userassist.txt

tips: Understanding these building blocks is essential for spotting how attackers abuse registry components for persistence (e.g., Run keys, Scheduled Tasks, ShellBags).
